message.content:
You are reviewing GitHub PR https://github.com/customink/pigment/pull/1139 by checking prior PRs for comments that may also apply.

PR Summary: Adds MiniProductCard and RecentlyViewedCarousel React components to `@customink/pigment-extensions/reco`. Modifies packages/extensions/reco/Atoms/Carousel/Carousel.tsx, packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/ProductsCarousel/utils.ts, and packages/extensions/reco/index.ts.

Steps:
1. Get changed files: `gh pr diff customink/pigment#1139 --name-only`
2. For each modified file (not new files), find recent PRs that touched it: `gh api "repos/customink/pigment/commits?path=<file>&per_page=5"` then for each commit SHA, check `gh api "repos/customink/pigment/commits/<sha>/pulls"`
3. For any PRs found, fetch their review comments: `gh api "repos/customink/pigment/pulls/<number>/comments"`
4. Analyze the comments to see if any past review feedback is relevant to the current changes

Return a list of past review comments that are relevant to the current PR's changes, and why they apply.

content.text: I'll systematically work through this review process.

{"body":"### Root `build` script breaks due to workspace ordering

**Medium Severity**

<!-- DESCRIPTION START -->
The `build` script runs `npm run build --workspaces --if-present`, which processes workspaces without guaranteed topological ordering. Since `extensions` comes alphabetically before `react` in `packages/*`, npm will attempt to build extensions before react. The extensions `TestingBanner` imports `Box` and `Typography` from `@customink/pigment-react`, so its `tsc` build requires react's `lib/index.d.ts` to exist — which it won't if react hasn't been built yet. The `build:packages` script correctly handles this by building extensions last, but the `build` script was not similarly updated.
<!-- DESCRIPTION END -->

<!-- BUGBOT_BUG_ID: 9b23bef1-943e-4346-80d8-5d27eb126c90 -->

<!-- LOCATIONS START
package.json#L8-L9
LOCATIONS END -->

content.content:
{"body":"### `onError` fires repeatedly on every parent re-render **Medium Severity** The `useEffect` that calls `onError` depends on `[isError, onError]`. Once `isError` is `true`, if the consumer passes an inline `onError` callback (new identity each render), the effect re-runs on every parent re-render and calls `onError` again. This can cause repeated error toasts, duplicate log entries, or other unintended side effects for a single fetch failure.
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/TrendingWidget/TrendingProductsCarousel.tsx","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### `imageSrcSet` not processed with design CID **Low Severity** When `imageDesignCid` is provided, `mapProduct` processes `image_src` through `processImageUrl` to apply logo-placement query params, but passes `image_src_set` through unmodified. If the API returns `imageSrcSet` URLs, the browser may select a srcSet image that lacks the design CID, showing an un-decorated product image on responsive or high-DPI displays.
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/api/mapper.ts","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### `isLoading` stuck true after mid-flight abort **Medium Severity** When `enabled` transitions from `true` to `false` while a fetch is in-flight, `isLoading` gets stuck as `true` permanently. The abort triggers an `AbortError` in the catch handler which returns without calling `setIsLoading(false)`, and the new effect with `!enabled` exits early without resetting state either. The widget then renders a `LoadingSkeleton` indefinitely. Notably, the sibling hook `useProductCardPriceDetails` avoids this by deriving `isLoading` from `enabled` state rather than storing it independently.
Additional Locations (1) - [`packages/extensions/reco/hooks/useTrendingProductsByCategory/useTrendingProductsByCategory.ts#L66-L68`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/37b055f2cb48bea53c05d69653d74744fac3043f/packages/extensions/reco/hooks/useTrendingProductsByCategory/useTrendingProductsByCategory.ts#L66-L68)
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/hooks/useTrendingProductsByCategory/useTrendingProductsByCategory.ts","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Duplicated price display logic and CSS styles **Low Severity** The formatted price rendering logic — calling `formatPrice`, checking `originalAmount`, and rendering the strikethrough-original / bold-discount / regular-price pattern — is duplicated between `RecommendationsProductCard` and `ProductCardPriceWithDetails`. The corresponding CSS classes (`.priceAmount`, `.originalPrice`, `.discountPrice`) are also duplicated across both CSS modules with identical definitions. This could be extracted into a small shared `FormattedPrice` presentational component.
Additional Locations (2) - [`packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.tsx#L41-L56`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/37b055f2cb48bea53c05d69653d74744fac3043f/packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.tsx#L41-L56) - [`packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.module.css#L8-L27`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/37b055f2cb48bea53c05d69653d74744fac3043f/packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.module.css#L8-L27)
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/ProductCard/RecommendationsProductCard.tsx","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Space key scrolls page on product card activation **Medium Severity** The `handleKeyDown` handler for the `role="button"` card activates `onClick` on Space key but doesn't call `e.preventDefault()`. This causes the browser's default Space-key scroll behavior to fire alongside the button activation, making the page jump when a user activates a focused product card with the Space bar. Per ARIA button pattern guidelines, Space on a `role="button"` element must not scroll the page.
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/ProductCard/RecommendationsProductCard.tsx","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Duplicated price display CSS across two components **Low Severity** The price formatting CSS (`.priceAmount`, `.originalPrice`, `.discountPrice`) and the corresponding JSX rendering logic for original/discount/normal prices are duplicated verbatim between `RecommendationsProductCard` and `ProductCardPriceWithDetails`. Both use `formatPrice` and render the identical conditional structure. Extracting a shared `FormattedPrice` atom would reduce the maintenance surface and ensure future price-display changes stay consistent.
Additional Locations (2) - [`packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/ProductCard/RecommendationsProductCard.module.css#L119-L133`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/4bc1defd2c759b81056013bf737755d25fd71bae/packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/ProductCard/RecommendationsProductCard.module.css#L119-L133) - [`packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.tsx#L43-L56`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/4bc1defd2c759b81056013bf737755d25fd71bae/packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.tsx#L43-L56)
 
","path":"packages/extensions/reco/Molecules/PriceDetails/ProductCardPriceWithDetails.module.css","user":"cursor[bot]"} [result-id: r4] [rerun: b10]

{"body":"@4thwithme @romachyzh @cyxn Please fill in actual values here. Open to name the directory however you like. Please provide real codeowners. These values are just suggestions, best-effort based on my context.","path":".github/CODEOWNERS","user":"teichmaa"} {"body":"### Root `build` script breaks due to workspace ordering **Medium Severity** The `build` script runs `npm run build --workspaces --if-present`, which processes workspaces without guaranteed topological ordering. Since `extensions` comes alphabetically before `react` in `packages/*`, npm will attempt to build extensions before react. The extensions `TestingBanner` imports `Box` and `Typography` from `@customink/pigment-react`, so its `tsc` build requires react's `lib/index.d.ts` to exist — which it won't if react hasn't been built yet. The `build:packages` script correctly handles this by building extensions last, but the `build` script was not similarly updated.
 
","path":"package.json","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### `.gitignore` will exclude `lib/` from published npm package **High Severity** The `packages/extensions/.gitignore` contains `lib/`, but `package.json` has no `files` field and no `.npmignore` exists. When npm publishes this package, it uses `.gitignore` as the exclusion list, so the entire `lib/` directory (containing all compiled JS and type declarations) will be excluded from the tarball. Since every entry in the `exports` map points to `./lib/...`, the published package will be completely broken. The root `.gitignore` already covers this path via `/packages/*/lib`, so the package-level `.gitignore` is both redundant and harmful. The sibling `packages/react` package avoids this problem by not having its own `.gitignore`.
Additional Locations (1) - [`packages/extensions/package.json#L4-L21`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/3608a6736f3f2a46ba3a3715a526afe76752c94d/packages/extensions/package.json#L4-L21)
 
","path":"packages/extensions/.gitignore","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Build missing CSS postbuild step breaks published package **High Severity** The `build` script runs only `tsc`, which doesn't copy or process CSS files. Unlike the react package (which has a `postbuild` step running `postcss` to copy/transform CSS into `lib/`), the extensions package has no such step. The compiled JS in `lib/` will contain `import styles from './TestingBanner.module.css'`, but no CSS file will exist there — causing a runtime module-not-found error for any consumer using the built output (including `demo-vite` via its `lib/` alias, and external npm consumers). The `postcss.config.js` is also unusable since `postcss`, `postcss-cli`, `postcss-custom-media`, and `@csstools/postcss-global-data` are missing from `devDependencies`.
Additional Locations (1) - [`packages/extensions/storefront/TestingBanner/TestingBanner.module.css#L1-L33`](https://github.com/customink/pigment/blob/c9caf6fd2838b415e108adc64bf67f2be147e98c/packages/extensions/storefront/TestingBanner/TestingBanner.module.css#L1-L33)
 
","path":"packages/extensions/package.json","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Build script silently drops mcp-server package from build **Medium Severity** The `build` script changed from `npm run build --workspaces --if-present` (which builds ALL workspaces with a build script) to an explicit list of specific packages and apps. This silently removes `packages/mcp-server` from the top-level `build`, even though it has a `build` script (`npm run generate && npm run compile`). The PR description only mentions "build extensions last in the chain" but the refactor also narrows the entire build scope, which could break CI/CD pipelines that depend on the top-level `build` command to produce mcp-server artifacts.
 
","path":"package.json","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"### Incompatible `@testing-library/dom` version with `@testing-library/react` **Low Severity** `@testing-library/react@^16.3.1` (resolves to 16.3.2) requires peer dependency `@testing-library/dom@^10.0.0`, but the `devDependencies` specify `@testing-library/dom@^9.2.0` (resolves to 9.3.1). This unmet peer dependency can cause test failures or unexpected behavior when running the extensions test suite.
 
","path":"packages/extensions/package.json","user":"cursor[bot]"} {"body":"We will update rules here `@customink/competitive-analysis` once we touch this. As of now, this team in GitHub seems abandoned, but at some point, we might need to fix it.","path":".github/CODEOWNERS","user":"cyxn"} {"body":"not competitive analysis anymore) "pricing and promotions"","path":"packages/extensions/README.md","user":"cyxn"} {"body":"### PostCSS glob processes CSS from node_modules **Medium Severity** The `postbuild` script uses the glob `'**/*.css'` which will match CSS files inside `packages/extensions/node_modules/` (confirmed: postcss-cli does not ignore `node_modules` by default). This will process third-party CSS through the PostCSS plugins and output it to `lib/node_modules/…`, bloating the build artifact and risking transform errors. The sibling `packages/react` correctly scopes its glob to `'src/**/*.css'`. The extensions package needs an equivalent scope, e.g. `'{storefront,reco,promotions}/**/*.css'`.
 
","path":"packages/extensions/package.json","user":"cursor[bot]"} [result-id: r5] [rerun: b11]

input.description: Extract clean description text from PR 1105 comments

content.content:
--- Comment 1 ---
The `useEffect` that calls `onError` depends on `[isError, onError]`. Once `isError` is `true`, if the consumer passes an inline `onError` callback (new identity each render), the effect re-runs on every parent re-render and calls `onError` again. This can cause repeated error toasts, duplicate log entries, or other unintended side effects for a single fetch failure.

--- Comment 2 ---
When `imageDesignCid` is provided, `mapProduct` processes `image_src` through `processImageUrl` to apply logo-placement query params, but passes `image_src_set` through unmodified. If the API returns `imageSrcSet` URLs, the browser may select a srcSet image that lacks the design CID, showing an un-decorated product image on responsive or high-DPI displays.

--- Comment 3 ---
When `enabled` transitions from `true` to `false` while a fetch is in-flight, `isLoading` gets stuck as `true` permanently. The abort triggers an `AbortError` in the catch handler which returns without calling `setIsLoading(false)`, and the new effect with `!enabled` exits early without resetting state either. The widget then renders a `LoadingSkeleton` indefinitely. Notably, the sibling hook `useProductCardPriceDetails` avoids this by deriving `isLoading` from `enabled` state rather than storing it independently.

--- Comment 4 ---
The formatted price rendering logic — calling `formatPrice`, checking `originalAmount`, and rendering the strikethrough-original / bold-discount / regular-price pattern — is duplicated between `RecommendationsProductCard` and `ProductCardPriceWithDetails`. The corresponding CSS classes (`.priceAmount`, `.originalPrice`, `.discountPrice`) are also duplicated across both CSS modules with identical definitions. This could be extracted into a small shared `FormattedPrice` presentational component.

--- Comment 5 ---
The `handleKeyDown` handler for the `role="button"` card activates `onClick` on Space key but doesn't call `e.preventDefault()`. This causes the browser's default Space-key scroll behavior to fire alongside the button activation, making the page jump when a user activates a focused product card with the Space bar. Per ARIA button pattern guidelines, Space on a `role="button"` element must not scroll the page.

--- Comment 6 ---
The price formatting CSS (`.priceAmount`, `.originalPrice`, `.discountPrice`) and the corresponding JSX rendering logic for original/discount/normal prices are duplicated verbatim between `RecommendationsProductCard` and `ProductCardPriceWithDetails`. Both use `formatPrice` and render the identical conditional structure. Extracting a shared `FormattedPrice` atom would reduce the maintenance surface and ensure future price-display changes stay consistent.
[result-id: r8]
[rerun: b16]
